source_old_req: The ADIRUp_process component shall enforce environmental assumptions on its inputs and outputs, specifically requiring that the six-channel acceleration inputs (acc1_input through acc6_input) be valid floating-point values sampled at a minimum frequency of 1 ms. The component shall guarantee that when erroneous action commands are received via the ADIRUp_error_action event input, the system responds by issuing abnormal alerts through the ADIRUp_error_out event output. The implementation layer shall ensure that data flows from the health management thread (HM_th) to the computation core thread (solve_th1) are properly structured, with raw data being filtered by the HM_th thread before being passed to the solver. Additionally, the implementation layer shall establish verification lemmas to demonstrate the system's fault tolerance, proving that the component will only exhibit common-mode failure and an erroneous display state (displayErroneousValue) when three or more input channels (e.g., 1-2-3, 1-2-4, 1-3-4, or 2-3-4) simultaneously report value anomalies (ValueErroneous), thereby verifying the system's absolute tolerance for single and double physical faults.
natural_requirement: The ADIRUp_process component shall define the implementation-level signal routing for ADIRUp_process.impl. In the implementation layer, ADIRUp_process.impl shall route data flow from acc1_input to HM_th.accData_in1 through C1 and data flow from acc2_input to HM_th.accData_in2 through C2.

requirement_items.text: The ADIRUp_process.impl implementation shall route the data flow from acc1_input to HM_th.accData_in1 through connection C1.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The ADIRUp_process.impl implementation shall declare connection C1 from acc1_input to HM_th.accData_in1.

requirement_items.text: The ADIRUp_process.impl implementation shall route the data flow from acc2_input to HM_th.accData_in2 through connection C2.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The ADIRUp_process.impl implementation shall declare connection C2 from acc2_input to HM_th.accData_in2.

quality_policy.traceable: Every item has trace_refs and expected_check metadata tied to AADL facts.
quality_policy.principle: Every generated sentence must name an explicit property assignment or structural connection; ordinary feature declarations are not used as rebuilt requirements.

natural_requirement_before_behavior_styl: The ADIRUp_process.impl implementation shall declare connection C1 from acc1_input to HM_th.accData_in1. The ADIRUp_process.impl implementation shall declare connection C2 from acc2_input to HM_th.accData_in2.

behavior_style_policy.basis: front-110 AGREE-derived requirements favor implementable behavior, constants, assumptions, guarantees, routing, and bounded properties.
behavior_style_policy.rule: Do not describe unsupported validity/legal-boundary semantics unless the AADL model exposes variables, values, or properties that can implement them.

natural_requirement_before_paragraph_rew: The ADIRUp_process.impl implementation shall route the data flow from acc1_input to HM_th.accData_in1 through connection C1. The ADIRUp_process.impl implementation shall route the data flow from acc2_input to HM_th.accData_in2 through connection C2.

paragraph_style_policy.rule: Req.txt is written as a natural-language requirement paragraph; per-item traceability stays in requirement_items.
paragraph_style_policy.basis: Front-110 AGREE-derived requirements generally combine function, assumptions, guarantees, and implementation notes into 1-4 sentence paragraphs.
